zotero.abstractNote: Logical fallacies are common in human communication, yet their automated detection remains an under-explored task in natural language processing. In this paper, we introduce the task of logical fallacy detection. We first curate a dataset of logical fallacies, LogiFall, which contains 1,900 examples of logical fallacies across 23 categories, collected from diverse sources including news, social media, and textbooks. We then propose a new model, FallacyDet, which leverages a combination of logical and linguistic features to detect fallacies. Our experiments show that FallacyDet outperforms strong baselines on the task of logical fallacy detection, achieving an F1-score of 0.68. We also conduct a human evaluation to assess the quality of our dataset and the performance of our model, demonstrating the potential of our approach for real-world applications.
